document_name: Official EU GDPR Text (General Data Protection Regulation)
description: Current EU GDPR regulation text and amendments relevant to processing of health data, consent, storage, transfer, and data sharing for human-subject research in Germany.
recency_requirement: Current version of GDPR text with latest amendments
steps_to_find: Access EUR-Lex portal for GDPR text
Identify relevant GDPR recital/Articles for consent, data minimization, access controls, and data sharing

document_name: Official German Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG)
description: German implementing law complementing GDPR, detailing national provisions for data processing in research within Germany.
recency_requirement: Most recent version including any amendments
steps_to_find: Visit gesetz-im-internet.de or bundesrecht.juris.de for current BDSG text
Cross-reference with GDPR requirements for health data
Extract provisions on consent, data security, and data subject rights

document_name: BSI IT-Grundschutz Standards for Health Data Security
description: Germany's IT security baseline standards applicable to handling health data, informing technical security controls and risk management.
steps_to_find: Download relevant control families (risk management, access control, encryption)
Map controls to project data flows and storage

document_name: University of Bonn Ethics Committee Submission Guidelines
description: Local ethics submission and approval procedures used by the University of Bonn for human subjects research.
recency_requirement: Current guidelines used by Bonn ethics committee
steps_to_find: Check University of Bonn Ethics Committee portal
Review submission templates and required documents

description: Open standard for organizing and describing EEG data that informs data management, sharing, and reproducibility across sites.
steps_to_find: Visit BIDS community website and GitHub repository
Review EEG-BIDS specifications for EEG, PSG, and metadata
Assess compatibility with planned data architecture

document_name: MNI Open Science/Open Data Policies (for reference)
description: Open science policies and frameworks illustrating data sharing, privacy safeguards, and data management suitable for neuroscience datasets.
recency_requirement: See latest published policies and practices
steps_to_find: Review official MNI Open Science pages
Identify data sharing practices and consent language
Note governance and privacy safeguards applicable to shared datasets
